definitions.definition: Haloalkanes are a class of organic compounds that contain a carbon-halogen bond. They are also known as alkyl halides. They are formed by the replacement of a hydrogen atom in an alkane by a halogen atom.
definitions.example: CH3CH2Cl is an example of a haloalkane.

definitions.definition: Haloarenes are a class of organic compounds that contain a carbon-halogen bond in an aromatic ring. They are also known as aryl halides.
definitions.example: C6H5Cl is an example of a haloarene.

definitions.definition: An alkyl halide is a class of organic compounds that contain a carbon-halogen bond. They are formed by the replacement of a hydrogen atom in an alkane by a halogen atom.
definitions.example: CH3CH2Cl is an example of an alkyl halide.

definitions.definition: An aryl halide is a class of organic compounds that contain a carbon-halogen bond in an aromatic ring.
definitions.example: C6H5Cl is an example of an aryl halide.

definitions.definition: Halogenation is the process of introducing a halogen atom into an organic compound.
definitions.example: The halogenation of methane forms chloromethane.

definitions.definition: Electrophilic substitution is a type of reaction in which an electrophile replaces a substituent group in an aromatic ring.
definitions.example: The electrophilic substitution of benzene forms chlorobenzene.

definitions.definition: An electrophile is a molecule or ion that is electron-deficient and tends to accept a pair of electrons.
definitions.example: A halogen atom is an example of an electrophile.

definitions.definition: A nucleophile is a molecule or ion that is electron-rich and tends to donate a pair of electrons.
definitions.example: A hydroxide ion is an example of a nucleophile.

definitions.definition: An SN1 reaction is a type of reaction in which a leaving group departs from a molecule and is replaced by a nucleophile.
definitions.example: The SN1 reaction of tert-butyl bromide forms tert-butyl alcohol.

definitions.definition: An SN2 reaction is a type of reaction in which a nucleophile attacks a molecule and replaces a leaving group.
definitions.example: The SN2 reaction of methyl bromide forms methanol.

definitions.definition: Stereoisomerism is the phenomenon in which two or more molecules have the same molecular formula and bond sequence but differ in their spatial arrangement.
definitions.example: R-2-chlorobutane and S-2-chlorobutane are stereoisomers of each other.

definitions.definition: A racemic mixture is a mixture of equal amounts of two enantiomers.
definitions.example: A racemic mixture of R-2-chlorobutane and S-2-chlorobutane is a mixture of equal amounts of the two enantiomers.

definitions.definition: An enantiomer is a molecule that is the non-superimposable mirror image of another molecule.
definitions.example: R-2-chlorobutane and S-2-chlorobutane are enantiomers of each other.

definitions.definition: Optical activity is the ability of a molecule to rotate plane-polarized light.
Haloalkane derivatives are compounds that are formed by the replacement of a hydrogen atom in a haloalkane by another group.
Haloarene derivatives are compounds that are formed by the replacement of a hydrogen atom in a haloarene by another group.
Alkyl halide derivatives are compounds that are formed by the replacement of a hydrogen atom in an alkyl halide by another group.
definitions.example: CH3CH2Cl is an alkyl halide derivative.

definitions.definition: Aryl halide derivatives are compounds that are formed by the replacement of a hydrogen atom in an aryl halide by another group.
definitions.example: C6H5Cl is an aryl halide derivative.
